note: Spec-owned. Removing, renaming, or adding a probe or route is a spec change. This file's SHA-256 is hard-coded as a constant in the test suite (RS-002) and appears in every evidence report. Expected completed set for a full run: 9 child probes x 2 routes + 2 controller-attested P01 instances + 2 shared probes = 22 composite-keyed entries.

route_plan.verdict_policy: per-route verdicts; no bare overall COMPATIBLE; one route failing while the other passes is a recorded per-route finding, not a veto (DD-001)

ownership.bootstrap_controller: mints run_id, creates run root, holds the single absolute /proc/uptime monotonic deadline through final aggregation, emits nonce-bound restore/build receipts; attests P01 per route
ownership.aggregator: validates receipts (never claims to have performed them); owns shared probes P02 and P04
ownership.route_children: own P03 and P05-P12 only; report only their route probes

entries.title: restore-identity (Route A partition: seam+harness+control plus shared contracts/aggregator/test projects)
restore-identity (Route B partition: seam+harness+control plus shared contracts/aggregator/test projects)
sdk-build-identity (build-time records; per-route runtime identity lives in P03)
loaded-assembly + runtime identity (set equality vs committed expected-loaded set; net10-on-net10 gate)
loaded-assembly + runtime identity (set equality vs committed expected-loaded set; net10-on-net10 gate)
solver-identity (non-discoverable install location, digest matches pin, banner 4.12.1)
options-manifest (frozen Option Manifest readback with normalization canary)
options-manifest (frozen Option Manifest readback with normalization canary)
closure-recovery (constant closure across both included-files option values)
closure-recovery (constant closure across both included-files option values)
